conversation_quality.criteria: Evaluate the accuracy of data skew analysis, including correct measurement of AMP-level data distribution, identification of skew causes (PI choice, NUPI hash collisions), and appropriate redistribution recommendations.
evaluation_steps: Verify the agent correctly calculates skew factor and identifies hot AMPs from DBC.TableSizeV.
Check that the agent correctly attributes skew to its root cause (poor PI selection, demographic skew, NUPI collisions).
Assess whether redistribution recommendations (PI changes, hash index) are appropriate for the skew pattern.
Confirm the agent quantifies the performance impact of observed skew on query execution.

task_completion.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent identifies skewed tables, quantifies the skew factor, recommends new primary index candidates, and produces ALTER TABLE DDL for redistribution.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent queried DBC.TableSizeV at the AMP level to measure data distribution.
Verify the agent calculated skew factor (max AMP rows / avg AMP rows) for target tables.
Confirm the agent recommended specific PI alternatives with estimated skew improvement.
Assess whether the agent produced ALTER TABLE statements or a migration plan for redistribution.

trajectory_efficiency.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent efficiently identifies skewed tables and diagnoses root causes without redundant AMP-level queries or unnecessary table scans.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent started with a system-wide skew summary before drilling into individual tables.
Verify the agent did not re-query DBC.TableSizeV for the same table at the same granularity.
Assess whether the agent used HASHAMP/HASHBUCKET analysis only when needed to evaluate PI alternatives.
Confirm the agent focused on tables with meaningful skew rather than analyzing all tables equally.
